title: Cyrtogonium diversifolium
taxonomy: Cyrtogonium diversifolium is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, including parts of Asia, Australia, and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Cyrtogonium diversifolium places it within the phylum Pteridophyta and the division Pteridophyta, with the family Thelypteridaceae being its most specific classification. The genus Cyrtogonium is part of the order Thelypterales, which is a group of ferns with unique characteristics. Cyrtogonium diversifolium's classification reflects its distinct features and evolutionary history as a member of this ancient and diverse group of plants.

intro: Cyrtogonium diversifolium, also known as the diverse-leaf fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Lomariopsidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
